location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday and Friday may vary

level_signals: Base salary range $202,000–$230,000 consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, specifically within search, retrieval, AI, or platform-centric products.
must_haves.qualifier: specifically within search, retrieval, AI, or platform-centric products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven ability to define a long-term platform thesis and translate technical constraints (APIs, LLMs, integrations) into a cohesive roadmap.
Experienced in working with engineering teams on complex, ambiguous systems where quality and performance are critical.
Data-oriented with strong product judgment, comfortable using metrics and experimentation to navigate uncertainty.
Exceptional communicator who can articulate technical strategies to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Demonstrates fluency in AI tools and emerging technologies, with a desire to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.
Passionate about enterprise software and inventing new workflows for the future of coordination.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with LLMs, RAG, search quality, or evaluation systems is highly preferred.

scope.surface: Agent Context — search, retrieval, and knowledge extraction systems powering Asana's AI experiences
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise work management platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Usage metrics for AI context features
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the multi-year roadmap for Agent Context, positioning Asana as the essential 'organizational memory' for the agentic enterprise.
Optimize retrieval quality and performance across search and RAG experiences, ensuring AI-driven results are comprehensive, relevant, and trustworthy.
Shape core AI capabilities that enable shared knowledge extraction, allowing humans and AI agents to coordinate work with full context.
Partner on governance and trust, working with cross-functional teams to ensure AI systems are dependable, safe, and enterprise-ready.
Translate complex data into action, using customer pain points and usage metrics to guide high-impact product decisions and roadmap prioritization.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Engineering, Design, and GTM teams to ship improvements that enhance the speed and utility of core AI experiences.
Communicate strategic vision, aligning stakeholders and leadership on technical tradeoffs and the path toward a more context-aware platform.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package alongside benefits

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (Agent Context, retrieval and search for AI experiences)
Explicit pay equity audit commitment mentioned
Well-defined in-office schedule (Mon/Tue/Thu) rather than vague hybrid language
Multi-year roadmap ownership signals high-impact senior role
red_flags: NYC office-centric hybrid with Mon/Tue/Thu in-office requirement eliminates fully-remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not addressed in JD — unclear for international applicants
Role spans search, retrieval, RAG, governance, and enterprise trust — broad scope may stretch one PM thin

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and 'enterprise' language)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: Friday work-from-home policy is conditional ('depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner'), making the exact hybrid cadence ambiguous beyond Mon/Tue/Thu.

The JD lists 'Familiarity with LLMs, RAG, search quality, or evaluation systems is highly preferred' — 'highly preferred' is treated as nice-to-have (soft) rather than hard required, though the strong language could indicate near-requirement.
